response.justification: The candidate is a strong senior individual contributor with 15+ years of software development experience and genuine developer-tooling interest (test runners, assertion libraries, CI/CD setup, MCP code generators), but this is a Level II people-management role requiring 2+ years of formal people management and 3+ years of technical leadership, and the resume shows no direct reports, performance management, or organizational leadership experience. Mobile exposure is limited to a brief iOS/Apple TV stint a decade ago, and developer-tools work is mostly side projects rather than 3 dedicated years. The gap on the core management requirements makes him a poor fit despite solid engineering credentials.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software development experience, well above the 8-year minimum
Authentic developer-tooling background: test runners (Trial), assertion libraries, CI/CD infrastructure, and MCP code generators
Teaching and community leadership: workshops, Selenium courses, code katas, and a long-running open-source platform as founder
concerns: No formal people-management experience — no evidence of direct reports, 1:1s, or performance management
Developer-tools experience is largely open-source side projects, not sustained professional focus in build/test infrastructure at scale
Mobile-platform experience is thin and dated (one iOS/Apple TV role in 2015-2016)

key_factors.factor: Deep hands-on engineering and testing-tools expertise
Limited mobile ecosystem and org-change leadership
